"cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-693", "name": "Protection Mechanism Failure"}], "description": "# Description

When using the sandbox with a `SourcePolicyInterface`, Twig does not always apply the sandbox restriction that forbids non-`Closure` callbacks for callback-accepting filters.

The issue affects the `sort`, `filter`, `map`, and `reduce` filters.

In the affected versions, the runtime check that rejects non-`Closure` callbacks in sandbox mode does not use the current template `Source`. As a result, when the sandbox is enabled through a source policy instead of being enabled globally, Twig can incorrectly treat the current execution as non-sandboxed for these callback checks.

This can allow user-controlled templates to pass arbitrary PHP callables to callback-accepting filters even though the template is being sandboxed through a source policy.

The issue happens when all these conditions are met:

- The sandbox is not enabled globally;
- A `SourcePolicyInterface` enables the sandbox for the rendered template;
- The template uses one of the `sort`, `filter`, `map`, or `reduce` filters;
- The callback is not a `Closure`.

# Resolution

The patch makes callback sandbox checks source-aware by propagating the current template `Source` to callback-accepting filters and using it when deciding whether sandbox restrictions apply.

# Credits

We would like to thank XavLim and Wade Sparks for reporting the issue and Fabien Potencier for fixing the issue.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00738, "percentile": 0.49703}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-05T20:41:33Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-24425"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-20T14:16:38Z", "published_at": "2026-06-05T20:41:33Z", "references": ["https://github.com/twigphp/Twig/security/advisories/GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-24425", "https://github.com/FriendsOfPHP/security-advisories/blob/master/twig/twig/CVE-2026-24425.yaml", "https://github.com/twigphp/Twig/releases/tag/v3.26.0", "https://symfony.com/cve-2026-24425", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/twig-x-x-sandbox-bypass-via-sourcepolicyinterface", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/twigphp/Twig/security-advisories/GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/twigphp/Twig", "summary": "Twig: Possible sandbox bypass when using a source policy", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-05T20:41:35Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2q52-x2ff-qgfr", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "twig/twig"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.16.0, <= 2.16.1"}, {"first_patched_version": "3.26.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "twig/twig"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.9.0, < 3.26.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Twig: Possible sandbox bypass when using a source policy
